diagrams.title: Basic Embedded System Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the fundamental components and their interconnections in an embedded system
diagrams.purpose: Understanding data flow and component relationships in embedded systems

diagrams.title: Memory Hierarchy in Embedded Systems
diagrams.description: Pyramid representation showing different memory types from fastest to slowest
diagrams.purpose: Understanding memory selection trade-offs between speed, size, and cost

diagrams.title: Microcontroller vs Microprocessor
diagrams.description: Comparison showing microprocessor requires external components while microcontroller is self-contained
elements: Microprocessor: CPU + External RAM + External ROM + I/O Ports
Microcontroller: CPU + Internal RAM + Internal ROM + Internal I/O + Internal ADC/Timer
diagrams.purpose: Understanding when to use microcontroller vs microprocessor in design

diagrams.title: Real-Time System Classification
diagrams.description: Classification of embedded systems based on timing constraints
elements: Hard Real-Time: Airbag, Pacemaker, Flight Control
Soft Real-Time: Video Player, Web Browser, Temperature Logger
diagrams.purpose: Understanding deadline constraints and system criticality

diagrams.title: Input-Output Signal Conditioning
diagrams.description: Shows how analog signals are processed through ADC to digital and vice versa
elements: Analog Sensors → Signal Conditioning → ADC → Processor → DAC → Signal Conditioning → Analog Actuators
diagrams.purpose: Understanding signal conversion and interfacing in embedded systems
